subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Two Particles P and Q — Direction, Same Direction, Speed & Position at Direction Change
stimulus: Two particles move along the $x$-axis. For $0 \le t \le 8$, the position of particle $P$ at time $t$ is given by $x_P(t) = \ln(t^2 - 2t + 10)$, while the velocity of particle $Q$ at time $t$ is given by $v_Q(t) = t^2 - 8t + 15$. Particle $Q$ is at position $x = 5$ at time $t = 0$.

parts.prompt: For $0 \le t \le 8$, when is particle $P$ moving to the left?
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $x_P'(t) = \frac{2(t-1)}{t^2-2t+10}$. Since $t^2-2t+10 > 0$ for all $t$, $v_P(t) < 0$ for $0 \le t < 1$. Particle $P$ moves to the left for $0 \le t < 1$.

scoring_points.description: Finds $x_P'(t) = v_P(t) = \frac{2t-2}{t^2-2t+10}$; sets numerator equal to zero

required_elements: Finds $x_P'(t) = v_P(t) = \frac{2t-2}{t^2-2t+10}$; sets numerator equal to zero

scoring_points.description: Correct answer with interval: particle $P$ is moving to the left for $0 \le t < 1$

required_elements: Correct answer with interval: particle $P$ is moving to the left for $0 \le t < 1$

parts.prompt: For $0 \le t \le 8$, find all times $t$ during which the two particles travel in the same direction.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $v_Q(t) = (t-3)(t-5)$; $v_Q(t) > 0$ for $0 \le t < 3$ and $5 < t \le 8$; $v_Q(t) < 0$ for $3 < t < 5$. $v_P(t) > 0$ for $t > 1$. Same direction: both positive on $(1,3)$ and $(5,8]$.

scoring_points.description: Finds $v_Q(t) = 0$ at $t = 3$ and $t = 5$; analyzes signs of $v_P(t)$ and $v_Q(t)$
Correct answer with analysis: both particles move in the same direction for $1 < t < 3$ (both positive) and $5 < t \le 8$ (both positive)

required_elements: Correct answer with analysis: both particles move in the same direction for $1 < t < 3$ (both positive)

parts.prompt: Find the acceleration of particle $Q$ at time $t = 2$. Is the speed of particle $Q$ increasing, decreasing, or neither at time $t = 2$? Explain your reasoning.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $a_Q(t) = v_Q'(t) = 2t - 8$; $a_Q(2) = -4$. $v_Q(2) = 3 > 0$ and $a_Q(2) = -4 < 0$: opposite signs, so speed is decreasing at $t = 2$.

scoring_points.description: Correct conclusion with reason: speed is decreasing at $t = 2$ because velocity and acceleration have opposite signs

required_elements: Correct conclusion with reason: speed is decreasing at $t = 2$ because velocity

parts.prompt: Find the position of particle $Q$ the first time it changes direction.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Particle $Q$ first changes direction at $t = 3$. $x_Q(3) = 5 + \int_0^3(t^2 - 8t + 15)\, dt = 5 + \left[\frac{1}{3}t^3 - 4t^2 + 15t\right]_0^3 = 5 + 18 = 23$.

scoring_points.description: Identifies first direction change at $t = 3$; sets up position

required_elements: Identifies first direction change at $t = 3$; sets up position
